title: 8 POS Adjustments and Traces
body_text:
8	POS Adjustments and Traces
8.1	Overview of POS Adjustments and Traces
Credit unions participate in the Point-of-Sale (POS) service either through the C2C Node, owned and managed by Central 1, or through the Cooperative Node, managed by the Canadian Credit Union Association (CCUA). POS transactions are routed through these nodes from merchant terminals facilitated by other
Interac
脗庐
member financial institutions to credit unions through various switch processing suppliers.
Valid types of POS transactions are:
Cardholder purchases
Merchandise returns
Reversals
Important
:	POS transaction records (paper or electronic) must be retained for seven years.
Central 1 provides daily settlement of POS transactions received from financial institutions, using information supplied by:
Cardtronics on behalf of the C2C Node
Everlink Payment Services on behalf of the Cooperative Node
8.2	Adjusting POS Transactions
A POS adjustment is required when POS transactions processed by the C2C Node or the Cooperative Node don芒聙聶t reconcile with those processed to your financial institution芒聙聶s banking system or if a customer claims a POS transaction is processed incorrectly.
The need for adjustment(s) can be identified by
the cardholder,
the network that processes
Interac
transactions,
the card-issuing financial institution,
the retailer financial institution, or
the retailer.
8.3	Requests for POS Adjustments from Customers
Financial institutions that receive requests for adjustments from customers must first investigate to determine if the adjustment can be initiated by the financial institution or if the customer should be referred directly to the retailer.
Where the amount processed to the customer芒聙聶s account differs from the amount on the transaction receipt provided by the retailer, you can initiate a trace request on the customer芒聙聶s behalf. See Section 9.2, Initiating a POS Trace Request. The customer must provide a copy of the retailer芒聙聶s receipt as proof of the discrepancy.
Where there is no difference between the amount processed to the customer芒聙聶s account and the receipt provided by the retailer, we recommend that you refer the customer to the retailer since any error (e.g., wrong transaction type, incorrectly validated amount) must be reversed by the retailer.
Note:	Advise customers that if a transaction under dispute involves cash back, the customer
must
be able to produce the cash register receipt
and
the POS transaction receipt issued by the retailer.
See the table below to determine the type of adjustment required.
Credit Required
Debit Required
An invalid or duplicate purchase is posted to the cardholder芒聙聶s account.
A purchase amount charged to the cardholder is greater than the actual purchase.
A purchase request was denied at the retailer芒聙聶s terminal but included on the switch reports and posted to the customer芒聙聶s account.
A merchandise refund was entered as a 芒聙聹purchase芒聙聺 in error.
A merchandise refund was reversed by the switch in error and therefore wasn芒聙聶t included in the totals.
An invalid or duplicate merchandise refund is posted to the cardholder芒聙聶s account.
A merchandise refund credited to the cardholder is greater than the actual refund.
A merchandise refund was denied at the retailer芒聙聶s terminal but was included on the switch reports and posted to the customer芒聙聶s account.
A purchase was entered as a merchandise refund in error.
A purchase occurred but wasn芒聙聶t included on the network reports or posted to the customer芒聙聶s account.
A purchase was reversed by the switch in error and therefore wasn芒聙聶t included in the totals.
A purchase amount wasn芒聙聶t posted to the cardholder芒聙聶s account.
A purchase amount debited to the cardholder is less than the actual purchase.
8.4	Processing POS Adjustments Received from other Financial Institutions
POS adjustments from other
Interac
financial institutions are contained in an administrative message file provided to Central 1.
Credit unions under the C2C Node
芒聙聯 Cardtronics processes debit and credit adjustment totals separately to your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 current account, daily.
Credit unions under the Cooperative Node
芒聙聯 Central 1 processes net adjustment totals to your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 current account, daily.
8.5	Effective Dates for POS Adjustments
All adjustments are credited or debited to your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 current account on the date they are processed by Central 1.
The following table summarizes submission, response, and limitation criteria pertaining to problem resolution. All 芒聙聹submit芒聙聺 dates are relative to the transaction date. All 芒聙聹respond芒聙聺 times are from date of receipt of trace request, claim, or debit letter (as applicable).
Note:	Unless explicitly stated as business days, calendar days are applicable.
